name: Olive Garden Pasta Frittata
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces Spaghetti; cook, cut 2 inches pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Beat eggs, half and half, cornstarch, salt, and nutmeg thoroughly until all ingredients are completely blended.
3. Blend the spaghetti pieces, green onion rings, and bacon pieces in a bowl until evenly mixed.
4. Coat a 1-1/2 qt round baking dish, including walls, with vegetable spray or margarine.
5. Empty the spaghetti mixture into the baking dish and spread evenly.
6. Add the Frittata batter to cover the filling mix.
7. Bake in a 350°F oven for about 25 minutes until the center is set.
8. When the center is set, cover the Frittata evenly with Fontana and bake until cheese is golden.
9. Turn off the heat and open the oven door. Let the Frittata set in the open oven for 15 minutes for the batter to set more firmly and make removing from the dish easy.
10. Before serving, sprinkle with Parmesan and cut into 4 wedges.
